back_translation: Do not harshly reprimand an elderly person, but speak to him as if he were a father; treat younger men as if they were brothers
Treat older women as if they were mothers, and younger women as if they were sisters, with all purity
Honor those widows who are truly widows

But if any widow has children or grandchildren/descendants, let them learn first to show godly-reverence to their own family, and to repay those who raised them, because such action is acceptable to Him before the presence of God
The widow who is truly a widow and has been left all alone, has placed her trust in God, and persists in petition-pleading and in prayer night and day
But the widow who lives for pleasure — even while still alive, is as if dead
Charge these things also, so that they may be without fault
But if anyone does not care for his own people, especially those in his own family, that person has denied the faith and is worse than one who does not believe
Let a widow be enrolled who is no less than sixty years old, and is the wife of only one husband

And one who is well-known for good deeds, such as raising children-and-grandchildren, welcoming strangers, washing the feet of holy-ones, helping those who are afflicted, and devoting herself to every good deed
But refuse to enroll younger widows, because when desires draw them away from Christ, they will want to remarry
They will receive censure, because they have violated the first promise that they made
At the same time, they will also learn to be lazy, going around from house to house, and not only lazy but also gossipy and meddlesome, speaking what should not be spoken
Therefore, I desire that younger widows remarry, have children, manage households, and not give the adversary opportunity to slander
Because some have already turned to follow Satan
If any believing woman has widows in her care, let her help them, and do not let the church have to bear the burden, so that the church may be able to help those widows who are truly widows
Church-elders who lead well are worthy of double honor, especially those who work hard in preaching and teaching
Because Scripture says, 'Do not muzzle the ox while it is treading out grain,' and 'The worker is worthy of his wages'
Do not accept an accusation against a church-elder, except if there are two or three witnesses
Those who continue to sin, rebuke them in front of everyone, so that the others may fear
I solemnly charge you before the presence of God and Jesus Christ and the angels-of-heaven whom He has chosen, to maintain these things without prejudice, and to do nothing with partiality
Do not lay hands on anyone hastily, do not have a share in the sins of others, keep yourself pure
Stop drinking only plain water, but drink a little wine, because of your stomach and the frequent illnesses you have
The sins of some people are clearly evident, going ahead of them to judgment; but the sins of some people appear only later
Likewise, good deeds are clearly evident, and even good deeds that are not prominent cannot be hidden
